title: Breno A. H. Marisguia
text:
I am a postdoctoral fellow in the Graduate Program in Political Science at the Federal University of Minas Gerais, where I also received my Ph.D. in Political Science; and a visiting researcher at the Institute of Applied Economic Research.
My research focuses on comparative politics, political institutions, and presidential interruptions in Latin America. I also work on research methods, epistemology, Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA), and computational tools that make social science research more transparent, systematic, and auditable.
Alongside my academic research, I have collaborated on applied projects concerning public-sector leadership, legislative-regulatory competencies, lobbying regulation, social policy evaluation, and democratic institutions in Brazil. I am also the developer of qcaERT, an R package for robustness assessment in QCA.

title: Breno A. H. Marisguia
text:
I am a postdoctoral fellow in the Graduate Program in Political Science at the Federal University of Minas Gerais, where I also received my Ph.D. in Political Science; and a visiting researcher at the Institute of Applied Economic Research.
My research focuses on comparative politics, political institutions, and presidential interruptions in Latin America. I also work on research methods, epistemology, Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA), and computational tools that make social science research more transparent, systematic, and auditable.
Alongside my academic research, I have collaborated on applied projects concerning public-sector leadership, legislative-regulatory competencies, lobbying regulation, social policy evaluation, and democratic institutions in Brazil. I am also the developer of qcaERT, an R package for robustness assessment in QCA.

title: Breno A. H. Marisguia
text:

Research methods

Presidentialism

title: Software
text:
qcaERT is a package for appraising robustness in Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA) (Ragin, 2014). Building on the workflow supported by the QCA package (Dusa, 2019), qcaERT treats robustness evaluation as an auditable process through diagnostics for calibration thresholds, truth table cutoffs, case composition, cluster heterogeneity, and theory-specific condition sets. The package aims to make robustness appraisal more detailed and transparent while remaining consistent with the epistemological underpinnings of QCA.

Repository: GitHub
CRAN: qcaERT
Recommended citation: Marisguia, B. A. H. (2026). qcaERT: Enhanced Robustness Tests for Qualitative Comparative Analysis. R package version 0.1.0.




Dusa, Adrian. 2019. QCA with R: A Comprehensive Resource. Cham: Springer. https://adriandusa.com/research/books/2019-QCA/
Ragin, Charles C. 2014. The Comparative Method: Moving Beyond Qualitative and Quantitative Strategies. Oakland, CA: University of California Press. https://www.ucpress.edu/books/the-comparative-method/paper

title: Software
text:
Apps

Research Design Notebook
The Research Design Notebook helps you organize a project by separating the main parts of a research design: the research question, the kind of knowledge claim, theory and concepts, empirical base, method, measurement, inference, and unresolved design questions. Also, it might serve as a neat teaching tool! The app is built so you can fill in your project information and then inspect it in different forms. The notebook, design map, memo, warnings, and downloads are generated from the same project info.

Link: Research Design Notebook
Recommended citation: Marisguia, B. A. H. (2026). Research Design Notebook. Version 0.2. Available at https://marisguia.shinyapps.io/ResearchDesignNotebook/
